cmd/jobs/entitlement.purpose: Cobra sub-command group for entitlement administrative jobs; exposes recalculate-balance-snapshots, which drives balanceworker.Recalculator to recompute metered entitlement balance snapshots and publish results to the event bus. All dependencies come exclusively from internal.App.

patterns.name: Source all services from internal.App
patterns.description: Commands never construct domain services locally; all dependencies (EntitlementRegistry, EventPublisher, Meter, NotificationService, Customer, Subject, Logger) are read from the package-level internal.App global.

patterns.name: Root + sub-command via cmd.AddCommand in RootCommand()
patterns.description: root.go defines RootCommand() returning the 'entitlement' parent cobra.Command; each job is registered through cmd.AddCommand(NewXxxCommand()) inside RootCommand().

patterns.name: cmd.Context() for context propagation
patterns.description: RunE closures pass cmd.Context() to all domain calls, never context.Background().

key_file_guides.role: Defines the 'entitlement' parent Cobra command and registers all sub-commands via cmd.AddCommand.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New jobs must be added via cmd.AddCommand here to be reachable from the CLI.

key_file_guides.role: Canonical job: builds balanceworker.Recalculator from internal.App fields (HighWatermarkCacheSize=100_000) and calls Recalculate(cmd.Context(), "default", time.Now()).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All RecalculatorOptions fields come from internal.App — adding a dependency requires updating cmd/jobs/internal/wire.go Application struct first and regenerating.

anti_patterns: Constructing domain services (Recalculator dependencies) inside the command instead of sourcing from internal.App
Adding business logic to RunE beyond orchestrating internal.App services

decisions.decision: Dependencies come exclusively from the internal.App global rather than local construction
decisions.rationale: Wire-generated initializeApplication in cmd/jobs/internal produces a fully wired Application; repeating wiring in job commands would duplicate provider graphs and risk inconsistency.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new entitlement job sub-command

cmd/jobs/internal.purpose: Single Wire DI entry point for the jobs binary: provides the Wire-generated Application struct, Viper config loading, and the package-level globals (App, AppShutdown, Config, ConfigFile) shared by every cmd/jobs sub-command package. Import direction is one-way outward — never import this from app/common.

patterns.name: Wire-generated initializeApplication in wire.go
patterns.description: wire.go declares the Application struct and calls wire.Build with app/common provider sets; wire_gen.go is the generated output. After any change to wire.go or constructors run 'make generate'.

patterns.name: Application struct as exhaustive service registry
patterns.description: Every domain service used by any sub-command is a typed field on Application; adding a dependency means a new field plus a matching common.* provider in wire.Build, then regenerate.
patterns.example: type Application struct { common.GlobalInitializer; BillingRegistry common.BillingRegistry; EntitlementRegistry *registry.Entitlement; ... }

patterns.name: Two-phase startup: loadConfig then initializeApplication
patterns.description: app.go's InitializeApplication calls loadConfig (populating Config) first, then Wire-generated initializeApplication(ctx, Config), storing App and AppShutdown. Sub-commands invoke it from PersistentPreRunE.

patterns.name: Package-level globals (nolint:gochecknoglobals)
patterns.description: App, AppShutdown, Config, ConfigFile are deliberate package-level globals consumed across all job sub-packages; the nolint annotation is intentional.
patterns.example:
//nolint:gochecknoglobals
var (App Application; AppShutdown func(); Config config.Configuration; ConfigFile string)

patterns.name: metadata() stamps service name 'jobs'
patterns.description: metadata() in wire.go maps config.Configuration to common.Metadata with service name 'jobs', stamping all telemetry.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "jobs") }

key_file_guides.role: Source of truth for which common.* provider sets are included for the jobs binary; declares Application struct fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a domain service to Application requires the corresponding common.* provider in wire.Build then 'make generate'.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire — DO NOT EDIT. Shows exact construction order and cleanup chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Re-run 'make generate' after any wire.go change; never hand-edit.

key_file_guides.role: Public InitializeApplication entrypoint called by cmd/jobs/main.go PersistentPreRunE.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters: loadConfig runs before initializeApplication; config validation failure aborts before Wire wiring.

key_file_guides.role: loadConfig loads and validates config.Configuration via Viper with config.DecodeHook() and SetViperDefaults.
key_file_guides.watch_for: viper.ConfigFileNotFoundError is tolerated (env-var-only config); all other read errors are fatal. Config.Validate() runs last.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the four package-level globals consumed by all job sub-packages.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Application fields must exist before any sub-command can reference them at compile time.

anti_patterns: Editing wire_gen.go directly instead of modifying wire.go and regenerating
Adding domain service construction logic in app.go or config.go instead of wire.go provider sets
Importing cmd/jobs/internal from app/common (creates an import cycle — direction is one-way outward)
Adding an Application field without a corresponding common.* provider in wire.Build
Calling viper.SetDefault directly instead of relying on app/config.SetViperDefaults

decisions.decision: All domain wiring delegated to app/common provider sets rather than inline construction
decisions.rationale: Centralizing providers in app/common means all binaries share identical wiring; constructor-signature changes surface at Wire generation time, not runtime.

decisions.decision: Package-level globals for App/Config rather than Cobra context values
decisions.rationale: Cobra's PersistentPreRunE runs before any sub-command RunE, making globals the simplest cross-package handoff without threading context through every layer.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new service to the jobs Application

cmd/jobs/ledger/backfillaccounts.purpose: Single-file Cobra sub-command that backfills missing ledger accounts (business + customer) for the default namespace. Intentionally bypasses the Wire DI surface to construct concrete ledger adapters, because the wired accountResolver is a noop when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: Bypass DI for the concrete ledger stack
patterns.description: newService() builds accountRepo, accountSvc, resolverRepo, and accountResolver directly from internal.App.EntClient; never uses the wired resolver from app/common (a noop / narrowed surface when credits are disabled).
patterns.example: accountRepo := accountadapter.NewRepo(internal.App.EntClient); accountSvc := accountservice.New(accountRepo, locker); accountResolver := resolvers.NewAccountResolver(resolvers.AccountResolverConfig{AccountService: accountSvc, Repo: resolverRepo, Locker: locker})

patterns.name: Flags in init(), logic in RunE
patterns.description: Package-level flag vars (createdBefore, customerPageSize, dryRun, continueOnError, includeDeleted) are registered in init() via Cmd.Flags(); RunE builds the service, resolves inputs, calls service.Run, prints summary, then exits non-zero on failures.

patterns.name: RFC3339 flag parsing normalized to UTC
patterns.description: Time-valued flags are strings parsed with time.Parse(time.RFC3339, ...) inside RunE and normalized to UTC.
patterns.example: parsed, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, createdBefore); parsed = parsed.UTC(); createdBeforeTime = &parsed

patterns.name: Print summary before returning error
patterns.description: printSummary is always called before any error return so partial results stay visible even on failure.
patterns.example: if err != nil { printSummary(output); return err }; printSummary(output)

patterns.name: Non-zero exit on FailureCount
patterns.description: After printing the summary, return a descriptive error when output.Result.FailureCount > 0 so the process exits non-zero.
patterns.example: if output.Result.FailureCount > 0 { return fmt.Errorf("backfill completed with %d failures", output.Result.FailureCount) }

key_file_guides.role: Single-file Cobra command: flag vars, init() registration, newService() building concrete ledger adapters, RunE orchestration over cmd/jobs/ledger/service, and printSummary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use internal.App globals; do not import wired app/common ledger services (noop when credits disabled). lockr.NewLocker must be created here and passed to accountservice.New (required for CreateCustomerAccounts). Namespace comes from internal.App.NamespaceManager.GetDefaultNamespace().

anti_patterns: Using the Wire-provided ledger account resolver from app/common (noop when credits.enabled=false)
Omitting lockr.NewLocker — accountservice.New requires a Locker
Parsing time flags outside RunE or skipping UTC normalization
Returning an error before printSummary (partial results become invisible)
Adding business logic in RunE beyond input construction, service.Run, and summary printing

decisions.decision: Build concrete ledger adapters directly instead of using DI outputs
decisions.rationale: AGENTS.md states backfills that genuinely need ledger writes must build concrete adapters directly because DI defaults are noops when credits.enabled=false.
Keeps the Cobra command thin and testable: the service package is pure Go with no Cobra/Ent imports, enabling unit tests without a real database.

code_examples.scenario: Construct the concrete ledger stack for the backfill
